summary: 119 tokens plus 32 light overrides, 100% description coverage, zero hardcoded colors across all three consumers (the scanner now audits the whole workspace, not just the playground). Contrast is executable: 54 WCAG checks across both modes, including the worst-case backdrop, run in npm run check and CI. Dark elevation was reworked this cycle: black drops cannot read on a near-black canvas, so shadows now render as light (a lit sky edge, a cool rim, a deepened drop), with light-mode values untouched. The docs consumer shipped a full art-direction pass on both tabs: dividers-not-boxes structure, editorial token tables, living type specimens, a single instrument bar with per-view indexes, and a scan-first copy voice for humans and agents. Documentation strings that print token values as content opt out of the coverage scanner via a same-line token-coverage-ignore comment.

priorities.title: Breakpoint tokens deferred
priorities.detail: 5 breakpoint values (767px, 768px, 1024px, 820px, 560px) in 22 media queries across consumers, most added by the docs redesign's responsive collapses. Not tokenized yet; revisit when the responsive strategy is defined.

priorities.title: Layout constraints are art-directed
priorities.detail: Hardcoded layout values (280px to 1600px) are playground-specific art direction. They don't warrant tokens unless a second consumer needs the same constraints.

priorities.title: Small structural px are intentional
priorities.detail: 1-6px micro-adjustments below the spacing scale (hairline offsets, badge radii). Tokenizing them would add overhead without design benefit.

priorities.title: Contrast over photography
priorities.detail: Resolved by the bounded backdrop system: a solid canvas scrim floor + photo capped at backdrop.photo-opacity (0.55 dark / 0.14 light) + tint. check-contrast.mjs verifies every panel text role against the worst-case backdrop in both modes; the docs backdrop section now demonstrates the caps live over the real banner photograph.

priorities.title: Visual regression baseline
priorities.detail: Verification is per-session via browser screenshots. The surface has grown again: light mode doubled it, and the docs art-direction redesign rebuilt both tabs. A Playwright baseline (deferred earlier) is worth re-costing.

priorities.title: Art-direction propagation to consumers
priorities.detail: The docs consumer shipped the dividers-not-boxes idiom end to end; photography-to-ui and switching-brain still use the card idiom the redesign retired. Scope the propagation with the maintainer before touching consumer chrome.

priorities.title: Desaturated dark-mode accent variant
priorities.detail: magenta-2 (#d60076) is 100% saturated and can vibrate against the blue canvas. The Material/HIG pattern is a slightly desaturated accent for dark themes. Taste-driven brand decision; explore separately.

priorities.title: Switching-brain paper mode
priorities.detail: The brain is pinned dark (glow-on-dark is the piece). A light variant means re-art-directing glow to shadow: a creative exploration, not a toggle.

strengths: Audit scanner covers every consumer via workspace discovery, the same auto-discovery as check:coverage; new consumers are audited with zero setup
Token accounting is structural: the scanner walks the DTCG tree, so base tokens (119), mode overrides (32) and per-leaf description coverage are exact
Light mode as sparse overrides: 32 tokens flip, derived tokens re-derive automatically via semantic var() references
Executable contrast contract: 54 checks per run, both modes, in CI; drift cannot land
Dark elevation renders as light (lit sky edge + cool rim + deep drop), so the shadow scale is actually differentiable on the near-black canvas, with real drops preserved for photographic backdrops
Mode parity validated: overrides must match base paths, must-flip list enforced, and mode overrides of extended tokens declare their own css extensions
Zero hardcoded colors in component CSS across all three consumers, including named colors
All text roles AA+ on every surface in both modes; interactive borders at 3:1 or better in both modes
The catalog dogfoods the system: live swatches, both bindings per flipping token, raw DTCG JSON one click away, and token $descriptions rendered as the documentation itself
100% token description coverage: every $value carries its own $description, so every token is legible to agents
Self-contained token package with fonts, ready for extraction
tokens/dist committed to git: both mode files available after clone, no build step
Bounded backdrop: text contrast over the photograph is provable in both modes, verified by the worst-case backdrop gate
Responsive AVIF/WebP pipeline: one mechanism (manifest + Picture/image-set), originals pristine, post-gallery payload down ~95%
risks: No automated visual regression baseline while the visual surface keeps growing (tracked as priority 5)
The accessibility and state tables count pattern occurrences, not correctness: they prove presence, not quality. Manual review still owns the difference.
